summary: Iteration 33 — Data Cleanup Control Panel end-to-end test. 24/24 backend pytest cases pass (collections listing, RBAC 403 for non super_admin, browse with filters, orphan scan, preview-delete, soft-delete with archive, hard purge without archive, archive list/restore/restore-idempotency, purge). Full frontend e2e flow as super_admin verified: page renders, collection picker works, search filter narrows rows, row checkbox + 'Type DELETE' modal (disabled→enabled at exactly 'DELETE'), Archive & Delete soft-deletes the row, Archive tab restore brings it back, hard purge also works. Supervisor (non super_admin) does NOT see sidebar nav item AND /collections API returns 403.
critical_code_review_comments: data_cleanup_router.purge_archive: `request: Request = None` is a workaround — FastAPI dependency injection will provide it; the None-guard is harmless but the default value is unnecessary and unusual. Consider declaring `request: Request` (no default) and ordering it before the Query param.

list_orphans loads up to 2000 rows into memory then nested-queries parent existence per row (N+1). For large collections (e.g. dprs, measurements) this can be slow. Consider batched $lookup or pre-collecting parent ids and a single $in lookup per parent collection.
browse_collection: `$regex` keyword search on un-indexed text fields will full-scan; OK for an admin tool but document the performance trade-off, or add a hard limit on `q` length.

ARCHIVE_TTL is metadata only — `expires_at` is computed but no Mongo TTL index is created on `cleanup_archive.expires_at`, so 'auto-delete after 30 days' will NOT happen unless a TTL index exists. Either create the index on startup or update the UI copy to say 'manual purge required after 30d'.
frontend purgeArchive uses window.confirm — fine, but Pure UX-wise it bypasses the typed-DELETE confirmation pattern used elsewhere. Consider unifying with the Confirm dialog for consistency.
test_credentials: super_admin: admin@erp.com / Admin@123; supervisor (403 RBAC check): supervisor@erp.com / Super@1234
seed_data_creation: Test creates TEST_uiseed_* / TEST_cleanup_* pr_categories via /api/procurement/master/categories and cleans them up via hard-purge through the cleanup endpoint itself. No residual seed data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration33_data_cleanup.py — set REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL before running. The suite is fully idempotent (creates its own seed, purges it). To rerun frontend, use Login.jsx data-testids (login-email, login-password, login-submit) and DataCleanup.jsx data-testids (cleanup-collection-select, cleanup-search, cleanup-apply-filters, cleanup-row-check-{id}, cleanup-open-confirm, cleanup-soft-toggle-bar, cleanup-confirm-input, cleanup-confirm-delete, archive-restore-{id}, cleanup-tab-archive/browse/orphans).
